message:
test(pages_bundle): pin integrity-manifest entry count + expected assets

test_bundle_generates_integrity_manifest previously used
`!manifest.files.is_empty()` with a hint message but accepted ANY
non-zero count of manifest entries. A regression in
src/pages/bundle.rs that silently dropped the embedded
PAGES_ASSETS loop would leave a 1-file manifest (payload only)
and still pass the assertion.

Upgraded to:
  - Assert manifest.files.len() >= 15. BundleBuilder embeds a
    fixed set of 15 static assets (index.html + 14 JS/CSS
    modules) from PAGES_ASSETS before the payload-chunk step, so
    fewer than 15 entries is definitionally a regression.
  - Iterate over a list of expected asset names (`index.html`,
    `styles.css`, `sw.js`, `viewer.js`, `auth.js`) and assert
    each is a key in the manifest. A regression that renamed or
    dropped any of these would now fire a clear per-file
    diagnostic.

Both assertions carry `got keys: ...` diagnostic output so the
failure mode points straight at the missing file rather than
requiring the operator to re-run and inspect the manifest
manually.

Validation: 41 tests in tests/pages_bundle.rs pass locally via
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_cass_pane3.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
